title: New chemiphotobleaching protocol for Raman spectroscopy
author: Elena Yakubovskaya
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bchqit5w

steps.title: Concentrate cells from fixed suspension onto GTTP 0
steps.instruction: Concentrate cells from fixed suspension onto GTTP 0.2 um membranes by filtering appropriate volume. (depend on cells density)

steps.title: Fix cells suspension with 2% final...
steps.instruction: Fix cells suspension with 2% final concentration of formaldehyde for 15 min.

steps.title: Put the filter in a small...
steps.instruction: Put the filter in a small petri dish and fill in with 2 ml of 3 % Hydrogen Peroxide.

steps.title: Put the petri dish with the...
steps.instruction: Put the petri dish with the filter under bright white (3695) direct light (60 Lumens per Watt) (regular desk LED light lamp) for 0.5 – 3 hours. The distance between the light source and petri dish should be 5-10 cm.

steps.title: Filter-Transfer-Freeze bleached sample to the mirror-finished...
steps.instruction: Filter-Transfer-Freeze bleached sample to the mirror-finished stainless steel microscope slide ( Taylor et al., 2017; dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.g4qbyvw ):

steps.title: Prepare filter wedges of GTTP membranes...
steps.instruction: Prepare filter wedges of GTTP membranes and mirror-finished stainless steel microscope slide:

steps.title: Place 5 ul sterile MilliQ water...
steps.instruction: Place 5 ul sterile MilliQ water on a clean mirror-finished stainless steel microscope slide and then wedge sample side down.

steps.title: Place mirror-finished stainless steel microscope slide...
steps.instruction: Place mirror-finished stainless steel microscope slide and filter on -80°C chilled aluminium block. After ~20 sec, peel membrane away and cells remain frozen to slide. Return slide to RT and air dry.
